title: How interest rates reshape farm management decisions this year
summary: Ag Marketing IQ In Depth: Ag economist David Widmar discusses how the cost of borrowing money impacts farm expansion and grain storage decisions. One tip: Fed action won’t solve the problem.

title: Bayer earnings top estimates on crop science, soybean gains
summary: Q1 adjusted EBITDA beats forecast by 14%. Soybean sales doubled on licensing payment from seed tech dispute and dicamba re-approval aids prices.

title: White Buffalo Girl’s gravesite cared for by community
summary: Down the Road: Residents of Neligh, Neb., have looked after an unassuming but decorated gravesite of a young Ponca girl since 1877.

title: Maryland farmer puts new technology to test
summary: Field Snapshot: As input costs surge, Chris Weaver manages his 1,000 acres with precision tech and 91-year-old wisdom.

title: Pest outlook 2026: What’s coming and what to watch now
summary: Wisconsin farmers face heightened pest threats this season. Stay ahead of these three early-season pests with scouting tips and timing strategies that can protect yield before damage takes hold in corn, soybean and alfalfa fields.

title: How does inflation impact land values?
summary: Land Sales: Farmland holds on to value and appreciation for investors — and farmers.

title: Hay season strategy: Plan now for shortage or surplus
summary: Beef Brief: Early planning gives you more options, whether you need to stretch your supply or put surplus to work.

title: Farm to fork: An inside look at pork processing
summary: Pork Pulse: Minnesota’s emerging pork industry leaders visit Hormel Foods’ flagship facilities, exploring the complete journey from producer to consumer.

title: Kansas land auction pace picks up through April
summary: Land Sales: Land with easy access to markets brings a premium at auction.

title: Nutrition is vital for heifer conception rates
summary: Hit pause before turning your bred heifers out to pasture. Ensure they have enough nutrients and don’t “starve” on lush grass.

title: Rice, soybean irrigation costs vary widely across eastern Arkansas
summary: University of Arkansas research maps the true cost of pumping groundwater across the state, revealing dramatic differences driven by water depth and crop type.

title: How to make more money by growing low-carbon crops
summary: Discover this and more at the TopSoil Summit, slated for June 10 at the Riverside Casino & Golf Resort in Riverside, Iowa.

title: How one tick bite sparked a new farm-to-table business model
summary: A Missouri couple turned personal health struggles into The Safe Spoon, a restaurant powered by local farmers and allergen-safe food.

title: Wheat rockets ahead on tighter supplies
summary: Afternoon Market Recap: Corn, soybeans also firm following USDA’s latest WASDE report.

title: Soybeans soar to 2-year highs as USDA signals tighter stocks
summary: Wheat hits two-year highs as drought slashes crop to 62-year low; corn tops $5.

title: House set to vote on summer E15
summary: Bipartisan bill may finally end temporary waivers and open year-round market for corn growers. Vote expected May 13 after multiple failed attempts in 2024-25.

title: Farm economy struggles as rural Main Street feels the pinch
summary: Farm Progress America: Creighton University's Rural Main Street Index hits contraction territory for 13th time since January 2025, signaling widespread economic pain.

title: Big, news-filled week ahead for farmers: Is your game plan ready?
summary: Morning Market Analysis: Corn, soybeans follow wheat, crude oil higher overnight ahead of USDA’s Supply and Demand update.

title: China eyes U.S. corn deal ahead of Trump-Xi summit Thursday
summary: Beijing in talks to buy American corn, sorghum, DDGs and soybeans during Trump's China visit.

title: Trump delays move to lower tariffs on beef imports
summary: Executive orders suspending tariff-rate quotas postponed amid outcry from ranchers and GOP lawmakers. U.S. cattle herd at 75-year low as beef prices hit records.
